revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Isono-jinja]][[en:Isono Shrine]][[ja:伊曽乃神社]][[simple:Isono Shrine]] {{Expand Japanese|伊曽乃神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Isono Shrine | native_name = 伊曽乃神社 | image = Isonojinzya20220226 6.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Isono Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|33.892717|133.187081}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu|ar|أماتيراسو|ary|أماطيراسو|az|Amaterasu|bcl|Amaterasu|be_x_old|Аматэрасу|be|Аматэрасу|bew|Amatérasu|bg|Аматерасу|ca|Amaterasu|cs|Amaterasu|da|Amaterasu|de|Amaterasu|el|Αματεράσου|en|Amaterasu|eo|Amaterasu|es|Amaterasu|et|Amaterasu|eu|Amaterasu|fa|آماتراسو|fi|Amaterasu|fr|Amaterasu|gl|Amaterasu|he|אמטראסו|hi|अमातेरसु|hr|Amaterasu|hu|Amateraszu Ómikami|hy|Ամատերասու Օմիկամի|id|Amaterasu|it|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|jaquote|アマテラス|jv|Amaterasu|ka|ამატერასუ|ko|아마테라스 오미카미|la|Amaterasu|lb|Amaterasu|lt|Amaterasu|lv|Amaterasu|min|Amaterasu|ml|അമതേരസു|mni|ꯑꯃꯇꯦꯔꯁꯨ ꯑꯣꯃꯤꯀꯃꯤ|ms|Amaterasu|nl|Amaterasu|nn|Amaterasu|no|Amaterasu|or|ଆମାତେରାଶୁ|pa|ਆਮਾਤੇਰਸ|pl|Amaterasu|pt|Amaterasu|ro|Amaterasu|ru|Аматэрасу|sco|Amaterasu|sh|Amaterasu|simple|Amaterasu Ōmikami|sk|Amaterasu Ómikami|sl|Amaterasu|sr|Аматерасу|su|Amaterasu|sv|Amaterasu|ta|அமதெரசு|th|อามาเตราซุ|tl|Amaterasu|tr|Amaterasu|uk|Аматерасу|uz|Amaterasu|vi|Amaterasu|wuu|天照大神|zh_min_nan|Amaterasu|zh_yue|天照大神|zh|天照大神|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} }} {{nihongo|'''Isono Shrine'''|伊曽乃神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Iyo Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Beppyo shrine|lt=Beppyo Shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社|mr|बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|simple|Beppyo shrine|zh|別表神社|qid=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} * {{ill|Q134917286|ltq=Q134917286|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q9610964|ltq=Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11378813|ltq=Q11378813|qid=Q11378813|1=List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province}} ** Series ordinal: 2 ** P155: {{ill|Q11523149|ltq=Q11523149|qid=Q11523149|1=Murayama Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Kuroshima Shrine|lt=Kuroshima Shrine|ja|黒嶋神宮|qid=Q11678192|1=Kuroshima Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q633945|ltq=Q633945|qid=Q633945|1=Saijō, Ehime}} * {{ill|Q907829|ltq=Q907829|qid=Q907829|1=Iyo Province}} * {{ill|Nii district|lt=Nii district|ja|新居郡|zh|新居郡|qid=Q11082347|1=Nii district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|33.892717|133.187081}} == image (P18) == * Isonojinzya20220226 6.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu|ar|أماتيراسو|ary|أماطيراسو|az|Amaterasu|bcl|Amaterasu|be_x_old|Аматэрасу|be|Аматэрасу|bew|Amatérasu|bg|Аматерасу|ca|Amaterasu|cs|Amaterasu|da|Amaterasu|de|Amaterasu|el|Αματεράσου|en|Amaterasu|eo|Amaterasu|es|Amaterasu|et|Amaterasu|eu|Amaterasu|fa|آماتراسو|fi|Amaterasu|fr|Amaterasu|gl|Amaterasu|he|אמטראסו|hi|अमातेरसु|hr|Amaterasu|hu|Amateraszu Ómikami|hy|Ամատերասու Օմիկամի|id|Amaterasu|it|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|jaquote|アマテラス|jv|Amaterasu|ka|ამატერასუ|ko|아마테라스 오미카미|la|Amaterasu|lb|Amaterasu|lt|Amaterasu|lv|Amaterasu|min|Amaterasu|ml|അമതേരസു|mni|ꯑꯃꯇꯦꯔꯁꯨ ꯑꯣꯃꯤꯀꯃꯤ|ms|Amaterasu|nl|Amaterasu|nn|Amaterasu|no|Amaterasu|or|ଆମାତେରାଶୁ|pa|ਆਮਾਤੇਰਸ|pl|Amaterasu|pt|Amaterasu|ro|Amaterasu|ru|Аматэрасу|sco|Amaterasu|sh|Amaterasu|simple|Amaterasu Ōmikami|sk|Amaterasu Ómikami|sl|Amaterasu|sr|Аматерасу|su|Amaterasu|sv|Amaterasu|ta|அமதெரசு|th|อามาเตราซุ|tl|Amaterasu|tr|Amaterasu|uk|Аматерасу|uz|Amaterasu|vi|Amaterasu|wuu|天照大神|zh_min_nan|Amaterasu|zh_yue|天照大神|zh|天照大神|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Q135037878|ltq=Q135037878|qid=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20060724063911/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp:80/db/jinja/560101.html == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 183246 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917280|ltq=Q134917280|qid=Q134917280|1=Kokuhei Chūsha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Unranked|lt=Unranked|ja|无位|qid=Q11504610|1=Unranked}} * {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|ko|종4위|zh|從四位|qid=Q11071127|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} * {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank|ja|正四位|ko|정4위|zh|正四位|qid=Q11123338|1=Senior Fourth Rank}} * {{ill|Q11071123|ltq=Q11071123|qid=Q11071123|1=Junior Third Rank}} == architectural style (P149) == * {{ill|Q930545|ltq=Q930545|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2500005003920 == Commons category (P373) == * Isono-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛媛県西条市中野甲1649', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Isono-jinja}} [[EN:Isono Shrine]] [[JA:伊曽乃神社]] [[SIMPLE:Isono Shrine]] {{wikidata link|Q11379396}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Isano Shrine |Image=[[Image:Isonojinzya20220226 6.jpg|270px|Shrine]]
Shrine |Location={{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|qid=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Saijo City|Q633945|qid=Q633945|1=Saijō, Ehime}}Nakano Ko 1649 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 degrees minutes, seconds|33|53|34|N|133|11|12.8|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined God={{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}{{ill|lt=Aratama/Japanese Spirit|Q495568|qid=Q495568|lt=Aratama|1=Mitama}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bukuni Kobetsu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shaku={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|lt=Meishindai|1=Myōjin Taisha}}) | Founded = (legendary) 13th {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|qid=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}7 years |Main shrine={{ill|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |Regular festival={{ill|lt=October 15th|Q2919|qid=Q2919|1=October 15}}・{{ill|lt=October 16th|Q2963|qid=Q2963|lt=16th|1=October 16}}|Map = {{Location map|Japan Ehime|lat_deg=33|lat_min=53|lat_sec=34|lon_deg=133|lon_min=11|lon_sec=12.8|label=Isono Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|53|34|N|133|11|12.8|E}}}} }} [[File:Isono 14.jpg|thumb|220px|'''Danjiri'' across the Kamo River]]'''Isono Shrine'' (Isono Jinja, {{old font|'''Isono Shrine''}}) is located in Nakano, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|qid=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Saijo City|Q633945|qid=Q633945|1=Saijō, Ehime}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}). {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=old shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q134917280|lt=Kokuheichusha|1=Kokuhei Chūsha}}, and currently it is {{ill|lt=Beppyō Shrine|Q10898274|qid=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} of {{ill|lt=Shinja Honcho|Q908077|qid=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmon|1=UNKNOWN}} is the "gosho car". It is known as the shrine where the largest danjiri is dedicated during the gorgeous "{{ill|lt=Saijo Festival|Q11628729|qid=Q11628729|1=Saijo Festival}}". In ancient times, it was also called ``Isono Shrine''. === Enshrined God === The following two deities are enshrined. It has two pillars and is collectively called "Isonojin" [http://www.isonojinja.or.jp/%E7%94%B1%E7%B7%92/ History] (official website). . :* {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}'s {{ill|lt=Aratama/Japanese Spirit|Q495568|qid=Q495568|lt=Aratama|1=Mitama}} :* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Bukoku Kobetsu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} Initially, there was only one deity enshrined, and it was called ``Isonokami''. According to the shrine's biography and old records, the first troupe was Amaterasu Omikami's Aratama, and later Bukoku Kobetsu no Mikoto was added to form two troupes. The main deity, Amaterasu Omikami, is enshrined in the center of the inner sanctum of the main shrine, and the side shrine deity, Bukoku Kobetsu no Mikoto, is enshrined to the left. === History === === Founding === According to the shrine's legend, in the 7th year of the 13th Emperor Seimu, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takekuni Kobetsu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (the prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}), the founder of Iyo Mimurabetsu, It is said that the development of the {{ill|lt=Toyo region|Q9067702|qid=Q9067702|1=Tōyo region}} began with the worship of {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, the {{ill|lt=emperor god|Q11581090|qid=Q11581090|1=Ancestors of the Emperor}}. It is said that Takekuni Kobetsu-no-Mikoto worshiped the spirit of Amaterasu Omikami and traveled to Iyo from {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|qid=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}, arriving at Mifunemori in present-day Sakuhi City, the same city (``Mifunemori'' is located along the Mifune River). During {{ill|lt=Emperor Kotoku|Q317980|qid=Q317980|1=Emperor Kōtoku}}'s {{ill|lt=Taika|Q1077930|qid=Q1077930|1=Taika (era)}} ({{ill|lt=645|Q31040|qid=Q31040|1=645}}-{{ill|lt=650|Q30179|qid=Q30179|1=650}}) There is a theory that the name ``Kamino District'' (later {{ill|lt=Arai District|Q11082347|qid=Q11082347|1=Nii district}}) came from the fact that gods were already enshrined at Isono Shrine and other places, and this area was called Kaminochi (land of the gods). === Outline === [[File:Isonojinzya20220226 4a.jpg|thumb|First torii and throwing stones and {{ill|lt=Isonojinzya|Q2744214|qid=Q2744214|1=Mount Ishizuchi}}]] In the ancient literature, in the 2nd year ({{ill|lt=766|Q30564|qid=Q30564|1=766}} of "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|qid=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}" {{ill|lt=Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181|qid=Q1373181|1=Tenpyō-jingo}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=766|Q30564|qid=Q30564|1=766}}), "Isonokami" was given the rank of {{ill|lt=Shinkai|Q11591025|qid=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}, which is lower than the junior fourth rank, and {{ill|lt=Kobe (Private)|Q11589711|qid=Q11589711|lt=Kobe|1=Kanbe (social class)}}It is written that 5 smokes will be used. “{{ill|lt=Shinsho Kokufusho|Q11502312|qid=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}” {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|qid=Q1373191|lt=In the 1st year of Daido|1=Daidō}} ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|qid=Q30021|1=806}}), 15 units from Iyo Province were allocated to "Isonokami" as Kanbe at that time; Ten of them are based on the sign of October of the first year of Tenpyo Jingo ({{ill|lt=765|Q31216|qid=Q31216|1=765}}), and five are based on the sign of December 3 of the second year of Tenpyo Jingo ({{ill|lt=766|Q30564|qid=Q30564|1=766}}). In the subsequent {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|qid=Q1151168|lt=Kokushi|1=Rikkokushi}}, {{ill|lt=Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} It is written that he was promoted to the lower rank of Sho4 in the 8th year ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|qid=Q23872|1=866}}), to the higher rank of the upper fourth rank in the 12th year of Jogan ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|qid=Q23629|1=870}}), and to the junior third rank in the 17th year of Jogan ({{ill|lt=875|Q23816|qid=Q23816|1=875}}).{{ill|lt=Extension (era name)|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Extension|1=Enchō}} In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, there is "Isano Shrine" in Arai District, Iyo Province. It is listed as ``Meishin Taisha'' and is listed as {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Eiji|Q1194167|qid=Q1194167|1=Eiji (era)}} ({{ill|lt=1141|Q19613|qid=Q19613|1=1141}}), he was conferred the rank of {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|qid=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}} and was given an imperial plaque by {{ill|lt=Emperor Sutoku|Q349287|qid=Q349287|1=Emperor Sutoku}}. In {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} in 13 years ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|qid=Q6736|1=1585}}), the shrine building and shrine treasures were completely destroyed by fire during Toyotomi Hideyoshi's {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikoku Conquest|1=UNKNOWN}}, and the shrine was relocated to Tosa Province to avoid disaster. {{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}}11 ({{ill|lt=1606|Q6747|qid=Q6747|1=1606}}) Returned to the old location. When the {{ill|lt=Modern Shrine Ranking System|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was established, it was not approved by the government as the enshrined deity was unknown, and it was designated as the First Prefectural Shrine of Iyo Province. {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} In 15 years ({{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|qid=Q18633|1=1940}}), his years of promotion campaign came to fruition and he was promoted to Kokupei Chusha. In recent years, people have been praying for {{ill|lt=International Organization for Standardization|Q15028|qid=Q15028|lt=ISO|1=International Organization for Standardization}} certification and permanence, a play on the phrase "ISO Shrine." === God level === * April 19, 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Tenpyo Jingo|Q1373181|qid=Q1373181|1=Tenpyō-jingo}} ({{ill|lt=766 years|Q30564|qid=Q30564|1=766}}), Junior Fourth Rank Lower ("{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|qid=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}") - Written as "Isonokami". * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 8th year ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|qid=Q23872|1=866}}) Leap March 7th, from the upper fourth rank to the lower rank of the fourth rank (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - Written as ``Isonokami.'' * August 28, 12th year of Jogan ({{ill|lt=870|Q23629|qid=Q23629|1=870}}), from Sho4i Lower to Sho4i Upper (``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - Written as ``Isonokami.'' * March 29, 17th year of the Jogan era ({{ill|lt=875|Q23816|qid=Q23816|1=875}}), from the top of the fourth rank to the third rank of junior rank ("Nihon Sandai Jirroku") - Written as "Isonokami". === Precincts === [[File:Isinojinzya20220226 1.jpg|thumb|Headquarters]] * Ichino Torii: Made of stone. The Stone Hammer God's Throwing Stone is on the right. * Ninotorii: made of copper * Stone pillar gate * In front of the Shinto gate: The Danjiri parade is performed in this space. * Hall of worship: The main hall of worship faces east because the enshrined deity is Amaterasu Omikami, but since the approach from the first torii gate is in the direction of the entrance from the city area, the approach and the main hall are at right angles. * Main shrine: Built in 1945. Shinmei-zukuri made of all-hinoki cypress. * Konohana Sakuyahime ({{ill|lt=Konohana Sakuyabime|Q1781862|qid=Q1781862|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}) Statue of Life: The second daughter of Oyama Gigami. Unveiled on March 18, 2013. * Mikoshi storage: Stores one adult mikoshi and one children's mikoshi. * Shrine: The sacred tree in front of the shrine grounds is 30 meters high and 5.5 meters in circumference, estimated to be 700 years old and is one of Saijo City's 50 famous water trees. Selected in March 1991. * Treasure Hall: 300 yen for adults, 100 yen for children * Isano Bunko * Funeral hall * A haiku monument: Kobayashi Issa's ``Is there a drop of flowers on my head?'' is along with a sub-inscription on the right just beyond the Ninotorii gate. ``Shinba ni Uchihaki Kuni de Hatsumode'' is on the left. ``Looking up at the glistening camphor tree, breathing in the breath as the mikoshi floats along the river, wearing Shichi-Go-San clothes with refreshing smiles,'' is located on the south side of the parking lot. * Parking: Large parking lot available and free of charge. Cannot be used during regular festivals. File:Isonojinzya20220226 5.jpg|Ninotorii and long approach File:Isonozinzya 0ɜ.jpg|Shinmon File:Isonojinzya20220226 3.jpg|Heiden, main shrine, and divine sandFile:Isono 0ɜ.jpg|Kohana Saya Hime no Mikoto Statue File:Isino jinja 20200101 02.jpg|Mikoshi warehouse File:Isino 04.jpg|Shrine office File:Isono 15.jpg|Otabisho File:Isonojinzya20220227 1.jpg|Issa Kobayashi's haiku monument File:Isonojinzya20220227 2.jpg|haiku monument === Setsusha == = [[File:Is0nozinzya 05.jpg|150px|thumb|Komori Shrine]] * Komori Shrine - Enshrined deity: Konohananosaku Yabiume no Mikoto Inside the shrine gate, from right to left facing the head office [[File:Isonojinzya202202026 2.jpg|150px|thumb|Tenma Shrine and plum blossoms]] * Tenma Shrine - Enshrined deity: Michizane Sugawara and others * Yohai Shrine - Yohai shrines in both Kamo and Kobe regions * Three shrines (Sanno Shrine, Itsukushima Shrine, Myoken Shrine) * Mizue Shrine - Enshrined deity: ancestral spirits and Kumon family ancestral deity * Guardian shrine (Matano Shrine) - Enshrined deities: Murakami Heibei, Takahashi Magobei, Takahashi Yaichizaemon * Gosaki Shrine - Deity enshrined: Sarutabikojin === Outside the border === * Isonomiya Otabisho: This is the place where mikoshi (portable shrines) stay during the annual festival, and where danjiri (danjiri) also gather. {{Wiki coordinates|33|54|10.69|N|133|11|19.74|E|type:landmark_region:JP-38|Map|name= Otabisho}} [[File:Isonozinzya 0ø.jpg|150px|thumb|Remains of Shimofuku Tumulus]] * Remains of the Shimofuku Tumulus: It is said that the interior was approximately 2 tsubo in size, with a large stone as the ceiling. === Cultural assets === ; Important cultural property * Yoshu Arai genealogy, 1 volume (ancient document) *: Ancient genealogy of {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}. The genealogy of the Arai clan, a powerful clan in the Iyo region, was copied around 1281 by {{ill|lt=Konden|Q3122771|qid=Q3122771|1=Gyōnen}}, a member of the family and a monk at Todaiji Temple. Together with the Wake genealogy ({{ill|lt=Onjoji|Q1756307|qid=Q1756307|1=Mii-dera}} collection in Shiga Prefecture) and the {{ill|lt=Akai family genealogy|Q11559330|qid=Q11559330|lt=Kaifu genealogy|1=Ama Clan Genealogy}} ({{ill|lt=Kagojinja|Q705126|qid=Q705126|1=Kono Shrine}} collection in Kyoto prefecture), it is called one of Japan's three greatest ancient genealogy trees. Designated on March 29, 1952 />{{PDFlink|[https://ehime-c.esnet.ed.jp/bunkazai/kennobunkazai/shiteibunkazai/shoseki/pdf-files/kuni/1-6-01yoshuniikeizu.pdf Yoshu Arai genealogy]}} (Ehime Prefectural Board of Education). . ;Tangible cultural property designated by the city * Isano Taisha Shrine Festival Picture Scroll === Reitaisai === ; {{ill|lt=Saijo Festival #Isono Shrine Festival|Q11628729|qid=Q11628729|lt=Isono Shrine Annual Festival|1=Saijo Festival}}October 15th and 16th "Encyclopedia of Annual Events" p48 First edition published on May 23, 1958 {{ill|lt=Masayoshi Nishikado|Q11629502|qid=Q11629502|1=Nishitsunoi Masayoshi}} Edited by Tokyodo Publishing. : {{ill|lt=Dashi|Dashi|qid=Dashi|ltq=Q11470974}} (comfort floats/stands) 77, {{ill|lt=Taikodai #Taikodai (overview)|Q11444734|qid=Q11444734|lt='''Mikoshi'''|1=Taikodai}} (Mikoshi floats. It is said that the number of floats dedicated to one shrine is {{ill|lt=List of Japan's #Festivals and Events|Q6636181|qid=Q6636181|lt=Most in Japan|1=List of records of Japan}}, and is unprecedented in other regions. Early on the 15th, ``Danjiri'' will enter the shrine and perform in front of our shrine gate. On the evening of the 16th, the festival ends when the ``Kami-san'' crosses the river (Kamo River, about 1km north of our company), and 11 ``Danjiri'' from the local Kobe area block the way of the ``Kami-san'' as they try to return home. File:Saijou matsuri isono.JPG|Scenes from the festival File:Saijoumatsuri Isono tsuitachi.JPG|Mikoshi music car File:Isono 11a.jpg|''Danjiri'' lined up on the banks of the Kamo RiverFile:Isono 12.jpg|'''Danjiri'''' File:Isono 13.jpg|'''Danjiri'' that blocks '''Kami-san'''' === Tradition === [[File:Isono 0ø.jpg|180px|thumb|Throwing Stone of the Stone Hammer God]] According to local legend, a long time ago, the goddess Isano and the male god {{ill|lt=Ishizuchiyama|Q2744214|qid=Q2744214|1=Mount Ishizuchi}} met on the banks of the Kamo River. The two fell in love, and the goddess forced them to marry. However, the male god refused, saying that he could not marry her because he had to continue his training at Mt. Ishizuchi. Furthermore, women are not allowed to accompany them on Mt. Ishizuchi, so they are not allowed to accompany them. ``Once I finish my training, I will get married, so I want you to wait until then. I will throw three large stones from the top of the mountain, so build a mansion where the middle stone falls and wait,'' he said as he climbed the mountain. Shortly after, three stones flew down from Mt. Ishizuchi, and Isano Shrine was built where the middle stone fell. Today, next to the first torii gate of Isono Shrine, there remains a stone that is said to be the throwing stone of the stone hammer god. === Famous worshipers === * {{ill|lt=1795|Q7815|qid=Q7815|1=1795}} ({{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|qid=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 7th year) - {{ill|lt=Issa Kobayashi|Q312709|qid=Q312709|1=Kobayashi Issa}}:{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shaso|1=UNKNOWN}} also has a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=haiku monument|1=UNKNOWN}} composed on that occasion. * {{ill|lt=1950|Q18597|qid=Q18597|1=1950}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}25) - {{ill|lt=Emperor Showa|Q34479|qid=Q34479|1=Hirohito}} * {{ill|lt=1982|Q2436|qid=Q2436|1=1982}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}57) - {{ill|lt=Crown Prince Naruhito|UNKNOWN|lt=Imperial Prince Komiya Naruhito}} ({{ill|lt=current Emperor|Q8195432|qid=Q8195432|1=Reigning Emperor}}) * {{ill|lt=2017|Q25290|qid=Q25290|1=2017}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}}29) - {{ill|lt=Emperor|Q208233|qid=Q208233|1=Emperor of Japan}} and his wife (currently {{ill|lt=Retired Emperor (Emperor Abdication Special Law)|Q62767620|qid=Q62767620|lt=Retired Emperor|1=Emperor Emeritus}} and his wife) === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === Related books === * Motohiko Anzu and Yoshihiko Umeda, editors and supervisors, "Shinto Dictionary", Jinja Shinposha, 1968, p. 9 * Edited by {{ill|lt=Eiji Shirai|Q11579387|qid=Q11579387|1=Eiji Shirai}} and Masanori Toki, "Dictionary of Shrines" {{ill|lt=Tokyodo Publishing|Q11524645|qid=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}}, 1979, p. 37 * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Saijo City|1=UNKNOWN}} "Saijo City Journal" reprint edition, 1991, pp. 51-57 === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Shozo Watanabe|Q515427|qid=Q515427|1=Shōzō Watanabe}} - The wedding was held at our company in 1956. === External link === {{commonscat|Isono-jinja}} * [http://www.isonojinja.or.jp/ Isono Shrine] - Official website * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/560101.html Isano Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection” {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Ehime Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Isono Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Isono Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Isono Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Saijo]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Ehime prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Saijo, Ehime]]
